news.title: 1966: The last time Argentina played Spain in the World Cup – match report
news.lede: The two heavyweights of world football have met only once at the World Cup, during the 1966 group stage. Having beaten England, Argentina will now face Spain in the 2026 World Cup final. Given that both teams regularly qualify for, and progress to, the latter stages of the World Cup, it is remarkable that the last time they met was in a group game at the 1966 tournament in England. Argentina won the match at Villa Park 2-1.

news.title: Why Fifa should bin off pointless World Cup third-place play-off
news.lede: Three days after the biggest loss of their careers, England’s squad have to go again and take on Kylian Mbappe’s France on Saturday in Miami. And Kieran Jackson argues: who even cares? From reproductive rights to climate change to Big Tech, The Independent is on the ground when the story is developing. Whether it's investigating the financials of Elon Musk's pro-Trump PAC or producing our latest documentary, 'The A Word', which shines a light on the American women fighting for reproductive rights, we know how important it is to parse out the facts from the messaging.

news.title: 40-year long Bayern Munich World Cup streak ended by cowardly England losing at the end
news.lede: Since 1982, Bayern Munich have had at least one player in the final of every single World Cup. With both England and France now eliminated in the semi-inal, Harry Kane, Michael Olise, and Dayot Upamecano have all exited the tournament. Just to recap, here is the incredible streak that Bayern Munich have maintained all these years, most of them coming courtesy of strong German performances at the biggest stage.

news.title: Spain at the World Cup 2026: Squad, fixtures, group and history
news.lede: Everything you need to know about Spain at the 2026 World Cup: their fixtures, squad, qualifying record, key players, coach and tournament history. European champions Spain are looking to complete an international double at FIFA World Cup 2026. Luis de la Fuente's side, led by captain Rodri, topped Group H after drawing with Cabo Verde before beating Saudi Arabia and Uruguay. They then eliminated Austria in the round of 32, Portugal in the last 16, Belgium in the quarter-finals and France in the semi-finals.

news.lede: The inside story of the campaign which promised so much before ending in familiar, agonising fashion.
